1.11a: Contact Completions Team at the address on the mortgage offer or instructions.
1.11b: Contact Completions Team at the address on the mortgage offer or instructions.
Provide recommendations for the correct documentation.
1.7: The lender does not allow lending when the borrower and mortgagor are different entities.
1.14: The firm may not act if the person handling the transaction or their immediate family is the seller.
1.15: The firm may not act if the person handling the transaction or their immediate family is the borrower.
3.1.3: Notification of the seller's legal representative is not required.
Conveyancer must ensure the seller's representative is registered with a regulatory body and holds a current practicing certificate.
3.1.4: Contact Completions Team at the address on the mortgage offer or instructions if the seller is unrepresented.
3.2.3: Notification of the seller's legal representative is not required.
Conveyancer must ensure the seller's representative is registered and holds a current practicing certificate.
3.2.4: Contact Completions Team at the address on the mortgage offer or instructions if the seller is unrepresented.
4.1: Valuation reports are provided electronically where possible.
4.3: Contact Completions Team at the address on the mortgage offer if valuation assumptions are incorrect.
4.5b: Certificate of title must be sent to the Completions Team at the address on the mortgage offer.
5.1.1: Residential remortgages require 12 months of ownership.
Buy-to-Let remortgages require 6 months of ownership.
Minimum ownership rules do not apply when exiting a Precise Mortgages Bridging loan.
Sub-sales and back-to-back transactions are not accepted.
Assignable contracts or irrevocable powers of attorney for intervening sellers are not accepted.
Freehold/superior leasehold acquisitions followed by immediate sub-leases to the borrower are considered sub-sales and are restricted.
5.2.1: Sub-sales and back-to-back transactions are prohibited.
Conveyancers must ensure compliance with the Economic Crime (Transparency and Enforcement) Act 2022 regarding the seller and associated parties.
5.4.4: Advise the lender of contamination revealed in searches only if it impacts lending decisions.
Japanese Knotweed Category C: Acceptable if not restricting access/amenity space and damage is minor.
Category C requires a transferable treatment plan from a Qualified Person (PCA/INNSA member) and a 10-year insurance-backed guarantee.
Japanese Knotweed Category D: Acceptable without a treatment plan.
Mundic Concrete: Acceptable only in Classes A, A/B, A1, A2, and A3.
Mundic Concrete Classes B and C are not acceptable.
Mundic reports must be addressed to the borrower or assigned to the borrower/mortgagee and dated after 1 January 1997.
5.4.5: Personal searches are acceptable if the firm follows the Search Code regulated by the Property Codes Compliance Board.
An unqualified Certificate of Title is required for personal searches.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted for remortgages only.
For Limited Company purchases from their own Directors, search insurance is accepted if deemed not a material risk.
5.5.3a: Report planning or building regulation breaches to the Completions Team only if the enforcement period is still active.
Notification is not required if the enforcement period has expired.
5.5.3b: Borrower should retain original planning permission.
Conveyancer must keep a copy on file.
5.5.3c: Borrower should retain original building regulation consents.
Conveyancer must keep a copy on file.
5.5.3d: Borrower should retain certificates of lawful use.
Conveyancer must keep a copy on file.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are acceptable if they do not exceed 10% of the total area.
Valuer must confirm the percentage of the flying freehold.
5.7.1b: Freehold flats are not accepted for Residential or Buy-to-Let lending.
For Bridging lending, freehold flats are accepted only if converted to leasehold simultaneously with completion.
5.7.1c: Report accepted flying freeholds or Bridging freehold-to-leasehold conversions to the Completions Team at the offer address.
5.8.1: Buildings with up to four flats are acceptable if the borrower occupies one and the freehold is charged to the lender.
If the borrower is a shareholder in a company owning the freehold, a stock transfer form must be held in escrow.
Additional charges on the freehold are required if owned by an entity connected to the borrower.
5.8.5: Charge is required over both the borrower's leasehold interest and their freehold interest in the reciprocal flat.
5.9.1: Commonhold is not accepted as security.
5.13.1: Deposits must be from the borrower's own resources without additional borrowing.
Non-interest bearing director loans are accepted for Limited Company BTL if the individual is a shareholder and the property transfer is at full market value.
Gifted family deposits under £20,000 require a deed of gift or letter dated within 3 months of completion.
Gifts of £20,000 or more must be reported to the lender if not already disclosed in the offer.
Deposit givers must be UK or EEA residents; funds must originate from UK or EEA.
Bankruptcy searches are required for all parties contributing to the purchase price.
Deed of gift indemnity insurance is required.
5.14.1: Leases must have at least 70 years unexpired at the time of completion.
5.14.9: Report ground rent if it exceeds 0.1% of the property value or purchase price.
Report ground rent if it increases by more than 25% upon review.
Report ground rent if review frequency is more often than every 25 years.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance for unsatisfactory lease terms is accepted if it covers the specific defect and meets Part 1 requirements.
5.14.12: Ground rent and service charges must be paid up to date at completion.
Receipts do not need to be sent to the lender.
5.14.13: Notice of assignment or charge must be served regardless of lease provisions.
Receipted copies of notices are not required by the lender.
5.14.15a: Lending on properties with absentee or insolvent landlords is limited to buildings with 6 or fewer flats.
Indemnity insurance must be in place.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance is required for absentee or insolvent landlords in buildings with 6 or fewer flats.
5.14.17b: Report remediation needs, fire safety issues, or non-qualifying leaseholder status to the lender.
Written instructions from the lender are required before completing if the building requires remediation.
Requirements apply to buildings 11m or higher, or with 5 or more storeys.
5.15.2a: Estate rent charges are acceptable if LPA 1925 Section 121 is excluded.
Acceptable if a mortgagee protection clause provides at least 28 days' notice before enforcement.
Acceptable if the rent charge owner is a management company owned by residents.
5.15.2c: Send signed blank stock transfer forms to the lender.
5.15.2d: Management company articles must not restrict the transfer of ownership.
5.17.5a: Send a certified copy of the Power of Attorney after completion.
5.17.5b: Send a certified copy of the statutory declaration of non-revocation after completion if applicable.
5.19.1: Lending is not available for affordable housing, shared equity, or shared ownership.
5.20.1: Report cases where roof space leases for solar panels do not meet UK Finance minimum requirements.
5.20.4: Existing Green Deal Plans must be disclosed.
6.3.1: Distressed sales, including sale and rent-back or arrangements to avoid repossession, are not acceptable.
6.4.4: Incentives must be deducted from the lower of purchase price or valuation for LTV calculation.
Cash or financial incentives are prohibited except for non-repayable family gifts.
Builder deposits are capped at 5% for residential purchases; not allowed for BTL.
Finders fees paid to third parties are not acceptable.
New build properties require a CML Disclosure of Incentives Form (DIF).
6.5.1: Vacant possession is required unless the mortgage offer explicitly permits a tenancy.
6.6.1: Tenancies for BTL products must comply with the lender's special conditions.
Distressed sales involving the vendor remaining as a tenant are prohibited.
6.6.4: HMOs are acceptable only if the offer includes 'HMO SPECIAL CONDITIONS'.
Requires proof of valid planning, certificate of lawful use, or permitted development rights.
Title must not have restrictions against HMO use unless covered by indemnity insurance.
6.7.1: Acceptable warranties include NHBC, LABC, Premier, Zurich Municipal, Checkmate, and several others listed.
Professional Consultants Certificates (PCC) are accepted if no warranty exists.
Retrospective warranties and retrospective PCCs are not acceptable.
6.7.4: Professional Consultant must have PI insurance for the greater of £500,000 or the property value.
6.7.6: The Professional Consultant's Certificate should be retained by the customer, not sent to the lender.
6.8.1: No advice needed for unadopted roads/sewers if suitable indemnity insurance is in place.
7.3: Occupiers aged 17 or over must sign a standard consent to mortgage form.
Forms for residential loans must be sent to the Mortgage Servicing Team in Wolverhampton after completion.
Consent forms are not required for Buy-to-Let applications.
8.1: Another lawyer in the same firm may act for a third party if there is no conflict of interest and independent advice is provided.
9.1: Indemnity policies should not be sent to the lender, but details must be provided with the Certificate of Title.
9.2: Indemnity insurance must cover at least 110% of the mortgage valuation.
10.2b: A minimum of 3 calendar days' notice is required for completion.
10.7: Delayed completion funds must be returned within 5 working days using the original payment method.
10.10: Funds can be held for a maximum of 5 working days (Advance Period).
If completion does not occur within the Advance Period, funds must be returned immediately.
10.11: Interest may be charged at the mortgage rate for the period the conveyancer holds the funds.
12.3.1: Bridging lending retentions/instalments are released to the borrower.
Residential and BTL mortgage facilities do not allow stage releases.
14.1.4: Form CH2 is required only for loans paid in instalments, deferred interest, or stage releases.
14.2.2: Post-completion documents required in electronic format: Certified PoA, non-revocation declaration, occupant consents, certified Mortgage Deed, Title Information Document/Register Completion Sheet, Title Indemnity Insurance, and executed stock transfer forms.
16.1.1: Title deeds are not retained by the lender.
16.3.7a: Contact Mortgage Servicing Team in Wolverhampton (0800 298 5714) for execution of transfer of equity.
16.3.7b: The attestation clause must state: 'Executed as a deed by [Name] as attorney for and on behalf of Charter Court Financial Services Limited in the presence of [Name].'
16.3.2: Contact Mortgage Servicing Team in Wolverhampton (0800 298 5714) for debt amounts.
16.5.3a: Send Deed of Variation to Mortgage Servicing Team in Wolverhampton.
16.5.3b: Send Deed of Rectification to Mortgage Servicing Team in Wolverhampton.
16.5.3c: Send Deed of Easement to Mortgage Servicing Team in Wolverhampton.
16.5.3d: Send Option Agreements to Mortgage Servicing Team in Wolverhampton.
17.1.1: Contact Mortgage Servicing Team in Wolverhampton (0800 298 5714) for redemption statements.
17.2.1a: Repayment remittance and discharge requests should be sent to the Mortgage Servicing Team in Wolverhampton.
17.2.1b: Discharges are processed directly with the Land Registry.
